rationale: The cited paper is used mainly as background in the nonlinear telecom pricing literature, appearing in grouped citations in the literature review. It is also cited once to support a general point about overage/underage economics (that higher-allowance plans can imply lower average per-minute prices). The citing paper does not build its identification strategy, model structure, or core empirical contribution on the cited paper's consumer learning framework, so the role is peripheral rather than central.
